If you're looking for information on Olea europaea (Olive tree), here is a paragraph about it:

The Olea europaea, also known as the Olive tree, is a perennial evergreen tree native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the family Oleaceae. The health benefits of the Olive tree include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as arthritis, cardiovascular disease, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of the Olive tree include oleuropein, a polyphenol compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as other flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins. The most common herbal preparations made with the Olive tree include olive leaf extract, olive oil, and herbal teas, which are commonly used in traditional medicine and dietary supplements.
